availability__employment_details.current_employment: Little Steps Learning Center, Lancaster, PA – Assistant Teacher (Toddler Room)

professional_development__program_knowle.explain_willingness_to_further_education:
I am very committed to continuing my professional growth in early childhood education and see this as a long-term career, not just a job. I already hold a B.S. in Early Childhood Education (PreK–4) from Millersville University, but I know that best practices in our field are always evolving, especially around trauma-informed care, inclusion, and play-based learning.

In the next 2–3 years, I plan to:
- Enroll in additional coursework or a graduate certificate focused on infant and toddler development and social-emotional learning. I am especially interested in courses that go deeper into attachment theory, responsive caregiving, and behavior as communication.
- Complete the full series of Pennsylvania Key online trainings related to health and safety, developmentally appropriate practice, and family engagement. I have already completed the basic orientation modules, and I keep a spreadsheet of trainings I want to take, including topics like supporting dual-language learners and working with children with sensory processing differences.
- Attend at least 2 in-person or virtual conferences or workshops each year (for example, PAEYC or NAEYC-affiliated events). I like coming back from conferences with concrete strategies I can try the next week, such as new transition songs, visual schedules, or ways to set up invitations to play.

Longer-term (5+ years), I am seriously considering pursuing a master’s degree in Early Childhood Education, Special Education, or Child Development, depending on where my experience leads me. I am especially drawn to learning more about inclusive practices so I can better support children with IEPs or developmental delays in a general classroom environment.

On a day-to-day level, I am very open to feedback and coaching. In my current position, I regularly ask my lead teacher and director to observe me during circle time or small groups and give me honest feedback on my tone, pacing, and classroom management. I keep a simple professional journal where I jot down what went well and what I want to improve (for example, how I handled a conflict over toys or how I supported a child who was having a hard drop-off). I would welcome opportunities at Aardvark Child Care to participate in mentoring, peer observations, or quality improvement projects related to Keystone STARS.

I am also willing to complete any trainings required by the program for licensing, Keystone STARS, or internal policies, even if it means attending evening or weekend sessions. I understand that maintaining high-quality care is directly tied to staying current on regulations, health and safety practices, and developmentally appropriate guidance strategies, and I am committed to putting in the time and effort to do that.
